commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-normalizable runtime tool schema quarantine before normal attempt and doctor normalization, expands focused tests, and replaces real-looking fixture names with synthetic ones.

PR surface: Source +275, Tests +393. Total +668 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirect still calls runtimePlan.tools.normalize on toolsRaw and bundled tools before filterRuntimeCompatibleTools, while the latest PR diff does not touch compact.ts.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can extend the provider-normalizable quarantine boundary to compaction and add compact regression coverage.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantines active runtime tools whose schema rows/descriptors are unreadable or unsupported before they reach provider/model normalization
- filters unreadable bundled MCP/LSP tool candidates before bundled allowlist/policy handling, then quarantines only post-policy active bundled tools
- adds doctor runtime checks using the same active tool projection path so unsupported active schemas surface before assistant turns
- keeps provider-repairable schemas available for provider normalization, avoids warning on disabled/policy-filtered tools, and uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 3 routed shards, 80 tests passed after the bundled allowlist fix
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- changed-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, too-close synthetic suffixes, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean after the bundled allowlist fix
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_5514a6cf76f4`, lease `cbx_26687ded98e8`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0 on rebased head `0e1425a1df7`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: active runtime tool schemas that would crash projection before an assistant turn are filtered/quarantined and surfaced through doctor diagnostics instead of poisoning the whole assistant startup path.

Real environment tested: fresh linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree from `origin/main`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate for the final rebased head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: cherry-picked and pruned the runtime/doctor slice onto fresh current main, handled the bundled MCP/LSP allowlist review finding, scrubbed synthetic test names, ran focused projection/quarantine/doctor/embedded-attempt tests, formatter check, diff check, changed-file scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unsupported schemas are quarantined before model session creation, unreadable tool rows do not crash explicit allowlists, unreadable bundled tool names do not crash bundled allowlist policy, disabled/policy-filtered tools do not produce quarantine telemetry, runtime quarantine logging avoids re-reading bad rows, and doctor reports active runtime schema findings through the same projection shape.

Observed result after fix: a malformed active tool is omitted from the model-facing runtime tool list and reported as a quarantine/doctor finding while healthy sibling tools continue.

What was not tested: no live Telegram/UI round trip for this slice; behavior is covered through embedded-attempt runtime tests, doctor tests, autoreview, and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
